title: equisetum arvense f. alpestre
intro: Equisetum arvense f. alpestre, also known as alpine horsetail, is a variant of the common horsetail plant (Equisetum arvense), a type of vascular plant that belongs to the class Equisetopsida. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of alpine horsetail include the treatment of urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and other kidney-related issues, as well as the improvement of bone health and the reduction of blood pressure. Alpine horsetail can be prepared for medicinal use in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the affected area.
